title: Robin Pronovost
content:
{{Top|fa|rwp|sprot}}
{{Otheruses|Robin}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:RobinPronovost.jpg]]
|name=Robin Pronovost
|birth=[[1975]]<ref name="Artist Series">{{SWArchive|url=eu/explore/profile/f20050818/index.html|text=''Star Wars'' Artist Series: Robin Pronovost}}</ref>
|death=
|listedpronouns=He/him<ref name="Pronouns">{{WebCite|url=https://flyingarmadillo.com/pronoun-update|text=Pronoun update|author=Pronovostm Robin|date=2024-07-20|work=[https://www.flyingarmadillo.com Art by Robin Pronovost]|archivedate=20250117165507}}</ref>
|nationality=[[Wikipedia:Canadians|Canadian]]-[[Wikipedia:Australians|Australian]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Multiple citizenship|dual citizenship]]}}<ref name="Australian">{{Twitter|artyewok|status/584204144237391872|Robin Pronovost|quote=2016 marks 20 years since I left Australia. Did you know I'm a dual Aussie/Canuck citizen|archivedate=20210404052512}}</ref>
|occupation=Artist<ref name="About">{{WebCite|url=https://flyingarmadillo.com/about|text=About|author=Pronovost, Robin|work=[https://www.flyingarmadillo.com Art by Robin Pronovost]|archivedate=20250418095225}}</ref>
|companies=
|website=[https://www.flyingarmadillo.com flyingarmadillo.com]
}}
{{Quote|Lab tech by day — artist when he has the time, Robin is like a superhero with amazing powers (Looks good in hats! Makes cake appesr!) and a secret identity, complete with lab coat and glasses. He is a trans-masculine artist based in the wilds of Canada.|Robin Pronovost's "About" description on his website|<ref name="About" />}}
'''Robin Pronovost''' is an artist who created ''[[Star Wars]]'' works from [[1999]] to [[2015]]. His art tutorials and artwork were published in ''Star Wars'' magazines and the Star Wars Kids section of [[StarWars.com]], and he contributed several hundred Artist Sketch Cards for multiple trading card sets released by [[Topps]].

Pronovost designed the [[Amaran]] and [[Bursa/Legends|bursa]] [[Species/Legends|species]] for [[Daniel Wallace]]'s cancelled reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Episode I]]''. Wallace and Pronovost collaborated again for the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] article "[[Castaways of Endor]]," with Pronovost both illustrating and co-writing.

==Biography==
===Overview of art career===
{{Quote|When I was a kid I loved ''Return of the Jedi'' because of the Ewoks. I was a total Ewok ''nut''. My brother, our friends, cousins and I would wrap towels around our heads (like Ewok hoods) and run through the woods with spears. I was always Teebo.|Robin Pronovost|<ref name="Artist Series" />}}
[[File:RobinPronovost-Jaxxon.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Art for the tutorial "Drawing [[Jaxxon/Legends|Jaxxon]]"]]
Robin Pronovost was born in [[1975]], and<ref name="Artist Series" /> his<ref name="Pronouns" /> interest in ''[[Star Wars]]'' began in early childhood. He would play with his cousin's ''Star Wars'' toys such as [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and saw [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] during its theatrical release. However, it was [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] and the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] featured in the movie that turned him into a fan; he saw that film multiple times in theaters and would play being the Ewok [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] with other children in his family. Pronovost considered becoming a [[Wikipedia:Veterinarian|veterinarian]] or an [[Wikipedia:Animator|animator]] before becoming a primarily self-taught artist, eventually taking independent classes and going to school for graphic design.<ref name="Artist Series" /> Although he is based in [[Wikipedia:Canada|Canada]],<ref name="About" /> he has dual [[Wikipedia:Canadians|Canadian]]–[[Wikipedia:Australians|Australian]] [[Wikipedia:Multiple citizenship|citizenship]].<ref name="Australian" />

As an artist for ''Star Wars'' publications, Pronovost illustrated online features, magazine articles, and trading cards.<ref name="About" /> In [[1999]], he began contributing artwork to two magazines that shared the title ''Star Wars Kids''.<ref name="Kids 5 1998">[[Star Wars Kids (1998) 5|''Star Wars Kids'' (1998) 5]] {{C|1998}}</ref><ref name="Kids 1 1999">[[Star Wars Kids (1999) 1|''Star Wars Kids'' (1999) 1]] {{C|1999}}</ref> His first contribution was the art for the "[[Star Wars License Plate Game|''Star Wars'' License Plate Game]]," which was written by [[Jef Czekaj]] and published in both the [[Star Wars Kids (1998) 5|fifth issue]] of [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|The Fan Club, Inc.'s]] [[Star Wars Kids (1998)|series]]<ref name="Kids 5 1998" /> and the [[Star Wars Kids (1999) 1|first issue]] of the [[Scholastic]] [[Star Wars Kids (1999)|series]] launched that year.<ref name="Kids 1 1999" /> The game involved placing descriptive phrases for five characters on the blank license plates of their associated vehicle or creature, such as "JEDI NME#1" ("Jedi Enemy #1") for [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] with his [[Bloodfin/Legends|speeder bike]]. Along with Maul, Pronovost's cartoons depicted [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] in his [[Anakin Skywalker's Podracer|podracer]], a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] riding a [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|STAP]], [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] by his [[Khetanna/Legends|sail barge]], and a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] wrangling a [[Dewback/Legends|dewback]].<ref name="Kids 5 1998" /><ref name="Kids 1 1999" /> In Scholastic's [[Star Wars Kids (1999) 4|fourth]] issue, Pronovost contributed that issue's "[[Star Wars Funnies]]," with cartoons of [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] as "Tar Tar Binks" and [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]] as "SeBULBa."<ref name="Kids 4 1999">[[Star Wars Kids (1999) 4|''Star Wars Kids'' (1999) 4]] {{C|1999}}</ref>
He also contributed the "Star Wars Funnies" feature published in both The Fan Club's [[Star Wars Kids (1998) 8|eighth]]<ref name="Kids 8 1998">[[Star Wars Kids (1998) 8|''Star Wars Kids'' (1998) 8]] {{C|1998}}</ref> and Scholastic's [[Star Wars Kids (1999) 8|eighth]] issues,<ref name="Kids 8 1999">[[Star Wars Kids (1999) 8|''Star Wars Kids'' (1999) 8]] {{C|1999}}</ref> depicting [[Palpatine/Legends|"Lord Sidious"]] shopping at the "Darth Mall," "Leonard [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]]" performing a [[Wikipedia:Vulcan salute|Vulcan salute]], and a toddler "Mini Maul."<ref name="Kids 8 1998" /><ref name="Kids 8 1999" />

Beginning in [[2005]],<ref name="Drawing Boga">{{SWArchive|url=kids/activity/draw/f20050325/index.html|text=Drawing Boga}}</ref> several art tutorials created by Pronovost were published in the Star Wars Kids section of ''[[StarWars.com]]'' as<ref name="About" /> "Learn to Draw"<ref name="Drawing Boga" /> and "''Star Wars'' Drawing"<ref name="Drawing Biohazard Suit Jar Jar">{{SWArchive|url=kids/do/draw/f20090325/index.html|text=Drawing Biohazard Suit Jar Jar}}</ref> features. His first tutorial was "Drawing [[Boga/Legends|Boga]]," the [[Varactyl/Legends|varactyl]] ridden by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref name="Drawing Boga" /> He was also profiled as part of the site's "Artist Series,"<ref name="Artist Series" /> and he created a printable Boga mask for [[Wikipedia:Halloween|Halloween]].<ref name="Revenge of the Halloween Masks">{{SWArchive|url=kids/activity/crafts/f20051026/index.html|text=Revenge of the Halloween Masks}}</ref> Another art tutorial, "How to Draw [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]]," was published in the "[[Padawan's Corner]]" section of the [[Star Wars Insider 92|ninety-second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine in [[2007]].<ref name="How to Draw Admiral Ackbar">{{InsiderCite|92|Padawan's Corner|How to Draw Admiral Ackbar}}</ref>

[[File:AshaDulok-SWG4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Art for the "Night of the Red Ghost" trading card]]
Pronovost was one of the artists who contributed Artist Sketch Cards to the [[Topps]] [[Wikipedia:Trading card|trading card]] set [[2005 Topps Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith|2005 Topps ''Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref name="2005 Topps ROTS">{{Topps|set=2005 Topps Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref> From 2007 to [[2012]], he contributed several hundred more Artist Sketch Cards for at least one Topps ''Star Wars'' trading card set per year.<ref name="2007 Topps 30th Anniversary">{{Topps|set=2007 Topps Star Wars 30th Anniversary|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref><ref name="2008 Topps TCW">{{Topps|set=2008 Topps Star Wars: The Clone Wars|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref><ref name="2009 Topps Galaxy 4">{{Topps|set=2009 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 4|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref><ref name="2010 Topps Galaxy 5">{{Topps|set=2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref><ref name="2011 Topps Galaxy 6">{{Topps|set=2011 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 6|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref><ref name="2012 Topps Galactic Files">{{Topps|set=2012 Topps Star Wars Galactic Files Series 1|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref> The base set of [[2009 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 4|2009 Topps ''Star Wars Galaxy'' Series 4]] also included his artwork on the card "Night of the Red Ghost," which featured the Ewok [[Asha/Legends|Asha]] preparing to strike a [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] with a [[Slingshot/Legends|slingshot]]. In Pronovost's quote on the card, he said, "I chose to show Asha protecting the woods from Dulok poachers. She is known as the 'Red Ghost' and is perceived by the Duloks as a forest spirit, rather than an Ewok, due to her ferocity. I really love Ewoks, and while the cartoon series was lighthearted for the most part, the [[Asha (episode)|Asha]] episode was serious and touching, for the most part."<ref name="2009 Topps Galaxy 4: Night of the Red Ghost">{{Topps|set=2009 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 4|cardname=Night of the Red Ghost|link=https://flyingarmadillo.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/star_wars_galaxy_4_base_card.jpg}}</ref>

In [[2015]], he produced a final sketch subset for [[2015 Topps Star Wars Rebels|2015 Topps ''Star Wars Rebels'']].<ref name="2015 Topps Rebels">{{Topps|set=2015 Topps Star Wars Rebels|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards}}</ref> Since his last work for ''Star Wars'',<ref name="About" /> he has become a [[Wikipedia:Medical laboratory scientist|medical laboratory technologist]].<ref name="Greetings">{{WebCite|url=https://flyingarmadillo.com/greetings-fellow-lab-folk|text=Greetings fellow lab folk!|author=Pronovost, Robin|date=2020-08-04|work=[https://www.flyingarmadillo.com Art by Robin Pronovost]|archivedate=20210305000628}}</ref>

===Amaran and bursa designs===
{{Quote|Early during the writing of the ''Essential Guide to Episode I'', I came across a George Lucas reference that the Gungans may have kept an army due to a past conflict with "bear-like creatures." Thinking this would be a cool thing to flesh out, I asked [Robin] Pronovost to come up with some designs. [His] creature, the bursa, didn't make it into this product but did appear as a dangerous antagonist in the computer game ''Galactic Battlegrounds'', and as a lavishly-illustrated beastie in the beautiful Wildlife of Star Wars. (Another of [Robin's] designs for the Essential Guide, the fox-like Amarans, also made it into the Wildlife book.)|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="danwallace blog 34">{{Blog|danwallace|34|Lostwords: The Essential Guide to Episode I}}</ref>}}
While author [[Daniel Wallace]] was working on ''[[The Essential Guide to Episode I]]'', an announced reference book that was in development from 1999 to [[2000]], he requested that Pronovost design the [[Bursa/Legends|bursa]] [[Species/Legends|species]] based on one of [[George Lucas]]'s undeveloped notes that a past conflict between the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] and "[[Bear/Legends|bear]]-like creatures" may have led the Gungans to maintain an [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|army]]. Pronovost also designed the [[Amaran]] species for the planned book<ref name="danwallace blog 34" /> when Wallace requested a [[Fox (creature)/Legends|fox]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species. The pair decided that this species should be called Amarans after "Amara," Pronovost's fox [[Wikipedia:Fursona|fursona]] that he had created as a university student<ref name="20th Anniversary">{{Twitter|artyewok|status/1387798855668965384|Robin Pronovost|archivedate=20210925084729|quote=Happy 20th Anniversary to The Wildlife of Star Wars and, by association, the birth of the Amarans, the fox aliens I designed. Here is a hilarious story about their genesis. A lot of this is in the wookieepedia article, but without my ADHD rambling. Strap in folks. 1/?}} {{C|thread}}</ref> and named after the character [[W:c:marvel:Amara Aquilla (Earth-616)|Amara]] from [[W:c:marvel:New Mutants Vol 1|''The New Mutants'']] comic books.<ref name="Amara">{{Twitter|artyewok|status/1378347742368567298|Robin Pronovost|archivedate=20210403140537|quote=Fun fact, I named Amara after Amara from the New Mutants comics because I am a gigantic nerd.}}</ref>

[[File:Amaran_concept_art.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The original concept art for the Amarans]]
The book was eventually cancelled, but Pronovost's work inspired the [[canon]]ical [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] depictions of Amarans and bursas in ''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]'',<ref name="Amarans">{{WebCite|url=https://flyingarmadillo.com/amarans-backstory-and-history|text=Amarans Backstory and History|author=Pronovost, Robin|date=2010-01-31|work=[https://www.flyingarmadillo.com Art by Robin Pronovost]|archivedate=20210123114514}}</ref> a [[2001]] reference book which was illustrated and co-written by [[Terryl Whitlatch]].<ref name="Wildlife of Star Wars">''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]''</ref> Whitlatch's version of bursas also appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'',<ref name="danwallace blog 34" /> a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] released in the same year.<ref name="Galactic Battlegrounds">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref> Pronovost later shared his original Amaran designs and non-canonical backstory on his [[Wikipedia:Blog|blog]] for personal use in [[roleplaying game]]s.<ref name="Amarans" />

Although it was noted on the [[Wookieepedia]] article for Amarans that there appeared to be a connection between the species and Pronovost's character Amara Fox,<ref name="Wookieepedia: JustinGann edit">{{WookCite|url=Special:PermanentLink/115796|text=Amaran}}, revision at 10:39 UTC, [[August 20|20 August]] [[2005]], by user {{U|JustinGann}}</ref> the artist previously denied it<ref name="Wookieepedia: Pronovost edit">{{WookCite|url=Special:Permalink/569586|text=Amaran}}, revision at 22:55 UTC, [[July 9|9 July]] [[2006]], by Robin Pronovost as an unregistered user</ref> and would claim that it was coincidental. His denials were due to concerns that attitudes towards the [[Wikipedia:Furry fandom|furry fandom]] would result in negative impacts on his professional career. When this did not occur as he had feared, he subsequently shared the truth through a series of tweets.<ref name="20th Anniversary" />

==="Castaways of Endor"===
{{Quote|Eight years ago, Dan Wallace approached me with an awesome idea - co writing an article about the inhabitants of the forest moon of Endor, based on Pablo's shipwreck colony theory. Being the [E]woks fan I am, I accepted and by [F]ebruary 2001, the article was approved, we had illustration pitch sketches out and then Star Wars Gamer ceased publication. D'oh.|Robin Pronovost, in a 2008 blog post about "Castaways of Endor"|<ref name="artyewok blog 1">{{Blog|artyewok|1|Castaways of Endor}}</ref>}}
[[File:Wistie_and_Ewoks.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Wistie/Legends|Wisties]] play with Ewoks in artwork from ''Castaways of Endor''.]]
Beginning in 2001, Pronovost and Wallace collaborated again on writing the article "[[Castaways of Endor]]," with Pronovost also providing illustrations for the story. The [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] cartoon was a favorite of both growing up, and they developed information and roleplaying details for several species, such as the Duloks, [[Jinda]]s, [[Fftssfft|dandelion warriors]], and [[Choreamnos|tree goats]]. The pair drew inspiration from the ongoing feature [[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life (Star Wars Gamer)|''The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life'']] in the magazine ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]''.<ref name="danwallace blog 120">{{Blog|danwallace|120|'Castaways of Endor,' New Article on StarWars.com}}</ref> The article would be based on a theory originated by<ref name="artyewok blog 1" /> [[Pablo Hidalgo]]<ref name="M. Hidalgo">{{Twitter|artyewok|status/1441385708556636160|Robin Pronovost|archivedate=20210924131543|quote=It is M. Hidalgo!}}</ref> that the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] was a "shipwreck colony."<ref name="artyewok blog 1" />

Pronovost was largely responsible for the sections on the Duloks and the Jindas. In researching the latter, Pronovost discovered that ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'',<ref name="Castaways of Endor thread">{{JCFCite|url=threads/29530570|board=Literature|thread=Castaways of Endor sees the light of day|archivedate=20190411164238}}</ref> a 2000 reference book by [[Bill Slavicsek]],<ref name="Guide to SW Universe">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> had described the Jinda leader [[Bondo]] as an Ewok despite numerous visual differences between the two species in the ''Ewoks'' cartoon. Additionally, Jindas were depicted with Ewok models in<ref name="Castaways of Endor thread" /> the [[2003]] video game<ref name="SWG">{{LucasArtsCite|url=company/release/news20030617.html|text=LucasArts Announces Star Wars Galaxies™: An Empire Divided™ Will Release June 26, 2003}}</ref> [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']]. The discrepancies were ultimately reconciled within the article<ref name="Castaways of Endor thread" /> by establishing that the Jindas seen in ''Ewoks'' were just one tribe out of several non-Endorian species that had been stranded on Endor, and that some stay-at-home Ewoks used the term "Jinda" as a disparaging term for Ewoks who were nomadic.<ref name="Castaways of Endor">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}</ref>

According to Wallace, the pair pitched the article for the ''Star Wars Insider'' and it was rejected, then it was repackaged for ''Star Wars Gamer'' prior to that magazine's cancellation; another ''Insider'' pitch was attempted before StarWars.com purchased it as a Hyperspace exclusive.<ref name="Castaways of Endor thread" /> "Castaways of Endor" was released on Hyperspace on [[December 1]], [[2008]].<ref name="Castaways of Endor" /> In a StarWars.com [[Star Wars: Blogs|blog]] post, Pronovost wrote that during the eight years it went unpublished, the article was in limbo in his e-mail archives and survived two [[Wikipedia:Hard disk drive failure|hard drive crashes]].<ref name="artyewok blog 1" />
